programme.desc: The Vibe is a 30-minute TV show that runs daily on weekdays on CGTN. The content focuses on exciting new trends, fashion, and cultural topics across a range of disciplines both in China and abroad. The host presents various culture-related topics in a fun way while also doing some in-depth analysis to give viewers a fuller picture as to what is happening, and why it matters.

programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already "living in the future"!

programme.title: Digits
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Connecting To The Future
programme.desc: Host Derek Muller escorts us to the physical heart of the Internet, and demonstrates how Internet co-creators Vint Cerf and Bob Kahn devised a language.

programme.title: Digits
programme.desc: We peer inside "The Internet of Things" and reveal how web advertising, search engines and 24/7 surveillance have transformed our lives.

programme.sub-title: S01 : E03 Guardians Of The Web
programme.desc: Muller introduces viewers to "white" hackers, the U.S. Secret Service’s cybercrime division including West Point soldiers working to protect us from the Web.

programme.desc: Increasingly high-tech tools are being used to evaluate youth baseball players. The question is can they outperform professional scouts?

programme.desc: Cosmologists were stunned to learn the universe is flying apart in all directions at an ever-increasing rate.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Relive the harrowing moments in Launch Control during NASA's second "Return to Flight" test flight .

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: As the clock ticks toward the critical Mars launch window, NASA scrambles to fix a critical space mistake.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: Despite multiple pressures, a shocking discovery causes NASA to cancel a historic launch.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.sub-title: S01 : E04 The End of Genesis
programme.desc: A spacecraft freefalls from orbit smashing into the ground when its parachute fails to deploy.

programme.title: Disasters in Space
programme.desc: The loss of Space Shuttle Columbia stands as one of the worst disasters in spaceflight history.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Cloud Cities of Venus
programme.desc: The surface of Venus is 95% carbon dioxide with an atmospheric pressure equivalent to 900 meters underwater.

programme.title: Exploring Venus
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Doing Science In Hell
programme.desc: Surface temperatures reach 450 degrees - any technology we send to Venus wouldn't stand a chance in the heat.

programme.title: Jim Al-Khalili's Guide to Life The Universe and Everything
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Origins of the Universe
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the first two great pillars of modern physics.

programme.title: Jim Al-Khalili's Guide to Life The Universe and Everything
programme.desc: We investigate the laws of physics that explain how life itself can exist in the universe.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: Scientists are exploring planets and moons that may once have supported life. Saturn's moon, Europa, is one.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: The necessary qualities for life may exist on planets outside our Solar System. These are called exoplanets,

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: The Nature of All Things
programme.desc: What is it that makes things what they are? Where does it come from? For ProfessorSeyyed Hossein Nasr, the quest for knowledge, specifically knowledge which enablesman to understand the true nature of things and which furthermore, �liberates anddelivers him from the fetters and limitations of earthly existence,� has been (andcontinues to be) the central concern and determinant of how we understand and live ourlives.

programme.title: Astrology Zone
programme.desc: Susan Miller is the founder and astrologist for AstrologyZone.com. The trusted,twenty-year-old platform provides the latest in intelligent astrological trends. Thisdiscussion with Deepak Chopra breaks down many popular misconceptions aboutastrological predictions, the various disciplines of study, ego, and consciousness inrelation to astrology. Miller also outlines the golden rule to understanding and getting themost out of astrological predications: astrology shows the condition, but not theoutcome�that outcome is completely up to you. �I can give you questions to ask to helpyou decide, but in the end, you write the script of your life.�

programme.title: Modern Metaphysical Idealism
programme.desc: Dr. Bernardo Kastrup, philosopher and author has been leading the modernrenaissance of metaphysical idealism. His theoretical reflections on the mind explainconsciousness as the only element of life that allows us to understand reality, andeverything we know about reality is subject to our experiences. This idea is often refutedby the thought that the brain is the source, not images and experiences provided byconsciousness.

programme.title: Nature, Space and Matter
programme.desc: Our understanding of the nature of time, space and matter have changed�even whatwe mean by these terms�in just the last few decades. Our interpretations continue tochange and more theories form as we learn and study these topics. Dr. Fred Alan Wolfexplains the origins of many of these foundational theories that have formed at differenttimes and why they came about. Before quantum physics, people knew that humanbeings were conscious. We knew that animals were conscious. But now, with newrevelations in quantum physics we begin to see a new role for consciousness.

programme.title: Creation Is Constant
programme.desc: Duane Elgin wants to show that the universe is a living field of existence, and that weare always in communion with a field of aliveness whether we are conscious of it or not.Throughout this lecture, Elgin explains his belief that in a living system, each part isintegral to the whole, so each entity is intimately connected to the entire universe. Tohim, creation is not something that happened with the Big Bang. Instead, creation isconsistently forming around us from a deeper abyss. The universe�s existence isbrought moment by moment through this continuous, moving process.

programme.title: The Sobras Institute
programme.desc: Kleopatra Ormos, M.D., Founder of the Sobras Institute, has spent her career refiningmethods that integrate principles of neuroscience, psychiatry, ancient medical andspiritual practices, nutrition, and exercise to help her clients improve their emotional andphysical health, and their personal and professional performance. The Sobras Instituteis a private research and teaching institute offering experiential workshops and privatecoaching for developing practical self-support skills for sustainable mental, emotionaland physical well-being. She discusses her research and life�s work at length in thisdiscussion with Deepak Chopra.

programme.title: Mind-Body Healing
programme.desc: In the �60s, James Gordon began self-seeking the broader dimensions of his mind and ifyoga, meditation and other methods to supplement his psychotherapy sessions, whichleft him satisfied but curious. He started taking it seriously for himself and the shift alsoreflected in his career. Dr. Gordon created ground-breaking programs of comprehensivemind-body healing for physicians, medical students, and other health professionals, andpeople with cancer, depression and other chronic illnesses. Mind-body medicine isbased on the scientific understanding of the inextricable connection between our innerselves and the social and natural world in which we live.

programme.title: Cosmopsychism
programme.desc: This episode of Curious Minds with Yujin Nagasawa discusses the unique idea of�cosmopsychism�; an idea which argues our personal conscious experiences aresegments of a larger consciousness that belongs to the cosmos. Yujin Nagasawa�s lifework lies in the mystery of consciousness, existence and the philosophy of religion. Thiscontroversial conversation with Deepak Chopra uncovers new theories andexplanations for consciousness, as they strive to uncover truth.

programme.title: Book Of Knowledge
programme.desc: Traveling the world 30+ years, David Verdesi finds himself back in Rome. Urged to uncover thereason behind the instinctive human desire for spiritual yearning, Verdesi starts retracing theintersection of science and theology within the city his family has called home since the early1400s.Vibrating from the walls of the city�s sacred spaces, he discovers findings of oral history,predating the beginning of humanity by thousands of years. Documents and rare rituals thatupend conventional ideas of the behaviors of the mind in ancient times. Narratives heavilyinfluenced by civilizations and cultures beyond Rome, continue to influence the moral compassof life today, Verdesi realizes he...

programme.title: Across the Ice
programme.desc: After 45+ expeditions throughout over 150+ countries, one of the world's leadingadventurers and visionaries of conservation, Swedish explorer Johan Ernst Nilsondawns upon his biggest adventure yet. Follow his journey across the planet from theNorth Pole to the South Pole in Pole2Pole. The 525 day mostly carbon-neutral trekcovers 20,000 miles by means of skis, dogsled, boat, bike and kite. Pole2Pole is notonly one of the most difficult adventures he�s ever attempted, but also one of his mostphilanthropic adventures. Johan visits charities along the way to share their stories andraise global awareness.

programme.title: Chasing Bliss
programme.desc: Water is life, and the great conductor that connects us all. The oceans cover over 70%of our planet and hold almost 97% of the earth's water. True bliss in constant movementgiving life around the globe. This is a story of an eclectic mix of people across Hawaii,Ecuador and the USA, who draw energy from this life force and channel it positively intotheir everyday lives. Join Paulino in understanding what drives Surfer Laid Hamilton, orfilmmaker Robert Greaves, Big-Wave World Champion Paige Alms and WindsurfingWorld Record Holder Sarah Hauser. Coffee Farmer Anti Clancy, Dr Greg Mitchell,Research Scientist & Algae Visionary at Scripps.

programme.title: Particle Physics
programme.desc: At only 21, Frank Wilczek, a graduate student at Princeton University, defined theproperties of color gluons, which hold atomic nuclei together while working with DavidGross. He is known, among other things, for the discovery of asymptotic freedom, thedevelopment of quantum chromodynamics, the invention of axions, and the discoveryand exploitation of new forms of quantum statistics (anyons).

programme.title: Consciousness of Culture
programme.desc: Bruce Parry highlights the moments of his life that formed his career and life passion tochampion indigenous cultures around the world through exploration and film. Bruce isinterested in altered states of mind, and the creation of myths, why do we think what wethink? And what life phenomena and environments sculpt these ideas?

programme.title: Borderline Consciousness
programme.desc: Berit Brogaard was the first researcher to show that consciousness comes in degreesand that there can be borderline cases of consciousness. Her areas of research includeperception, consciousness, emotions, philosophical psychology, semantics andphilosophical logic. She also researches synesthesia, savant syndrome, blind sight, andperceptual reports.

programme.title: The Knobe Effect
programme.desc: We have experiences, creativity, understanding, thoughts, feelings and desires but dowe know how these occurrences happen? Joshua Knobe�s research has beenconcerned with the impact of people's moral judgments on their intuitions about thisquestion. He believes strongly that people's intuitions about whether a particular entity isconscious, essentially depends on whether the entity has the right sort of body.Therefore, he believes embodiment shapes our view of what is reality.

programme.title: Quantifying Beauty
programme.desc: How do we scientifically quantify beauty in people and in nature? How does the worldembody beautiful ideas? Dr. Frank Wilczek�s conversation with Deepak Chopradiscusses his book, �A Beautiful Question� which tests the common misconceptionsabout how we form our opinions on success, beauty and our imaginations.

programme.title: Connections Between Science & Philosophy
programme.desc: Dr. Menas Kafatos is internationally known for bridging the disciplines, writing andlecturing extensively on the convergence between science and philosophy, as there isan inherent need for a dialogue between science, spirituality, and religion. His book, Creating Cosmos with Deepak Chopra discusses the melding of topics like inflation,environmental issues, natural hazards, quantum theory and consciousness.

programme.title: Iranian Space Pioneer
programme.desc: Iranian electrical engineer, entrepreneur and former astronaut, Anousheh Ansari wasthe first female private space explorer to fly to the International Space Station. Sheexplains the challenging journey and the determination needed to reach space afterimmigrating to the U.S. from Iran. She also became the first person to publish a blogfrom space. The content invited readers to share her experience with the world. Shedescribed the Russian Soyuz capsule during liftoff, talked about the sights and smells ofthe space station, and explained the intricacies of everyday activities.

programme.title: Philosopher of the Mind
programme.desc: Dr. Paavo Pylkk�nen�s eclectic approach combines new physics-based insights withthose of analytical philosophy, phenomenology, cognitive science and neuroscience. Heproposes a view in which the mechanistic framework of classical physics andneuroscience is complemented by a more holistic approach. Pylkk�nen is particularlyknown for his work on mind-body-studies, building on David Bohm's interpretation ofquantum mechanics.

programme.title: Evolution of Ideas
programme.desc: Everything we can know about our lives and the universe is a subjective experience,either formed by one's own perceptions or the reports of others. Dr. Bernardo Kastrupexplains how we commonly associate this understanding about the universe assomething separate of our consciousness, however he strongly believes otherwise;Kastrup believes the evolution of ideas we perceive about the universe are actuallyoriginating within us.

programme.title: Quanzhou Puppet Theater: China’s "Pinocchio Adventure"
programme.desc: In the film, the puppet Pinocchio, carved and created by an old carpenter, comes to life as a real human. Puppets are lifeless by nature—it is the masterful skills of their puppeteers’ fingertips that breathe life into them. What does China’s own "Pinocchio Adventure" look like? This episode takes you into the world of Quanzhou String Puppet Theater. Explore behind-the-scenes stories, from the intricate carving of puppet heads to the art of puppet performance, and witness how the dance of ten fingers and threads weaves a thousand vivid characters to life!

programme.title: China Untapped: Changsha
programme.desc: The city of Changsha is one of central China's powerhouses. It's a leader in both the construction machinery industry and in media – Changsha was China's first UNESCO "Creative City". It's also one of China's best-known cities for its nightlife. The soul of a city is in its people, and this episode focuses on the women of Changsha who are helping to innovate, change and further develop this city they call home.

programme.title: China Untapped: Changsha
programme.desc: The city of Changsha is one of central China's powerhouses. It's a leader in both the construction machinery industry and in media – Changsha was China's first UNESCO "Creative City". It's also one of China's best-known cities for its nightlife. The soul of a city is in its people, and this episode focuses on the women of Changsha who are helping to innovate, change and further develop this city they call home.

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. Guided by the concept of "lucid waters and lush mountains are invaluable assets", Huzhou now is committed to developing green industries. In this episode, CGTN hosts Tianran He and Hannah Wilson travelled to Huzhou, where they tried a variety of guesthouse hotels in the Moganshan Mountains; experienced the life of digital nomads in Anji’s countryside; learned about the eco-concepts behind China's traditional architectures in Nanxun Ancient Town; and explored the development of eco-agriculture powered by high-tech along...

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. programme.title: China Bound：Xiamen
programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

programme.title: China Bound：Xiamen
programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

programme.title: China Bound：Suzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He visits Suzhou, a historic city in eastern China famously admired by Marco Polo. Crisscrossed by canals, Suzhou is renowned for its exquisite gardens, where ancient scholars recreated nature's beauty within their own homes. Tianran discovers a city of refined tastes, where even a bowl of breakfast noodles comes with unspoken etiquette, locals insist on only in-season produce, and foodies eagerly await the arrival of prized hairy crabs.

programme.title: China Bound：Suzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He visits Suzhou, a historic city in eastern China famously admired by Marco Polo. Crisscrossed by canals, Suzhou is renowned for its exquisite gardens, where ancient scholars recreated nature's beauty within their own homes. Tianran discovers a city of refined tastes, where even a bowl of breakfast noodles comes with unspoken etiquette, locals insist on only in-season produce, and foodies eagerly await the arrival of prized hairy crabs.

programme.title: China Bound: Chengdu
programme.desc: Tianran spends 3 days in Chengdu, the capital of Sichuan Province and UNESCO's first City of Gastronomy. He dives deep into Chengdu culture, exploring the infamous "mala" numbing and spicy flavors of Sichuanese street food and hotpot, and visiting historical attractions like Wuhou Shrine, Du Fu's Thatched Cottage, and the Jinsha Site Museum, which houses exquisite relics from a 3,000-year-old kingdom uncovered beneath the streets of Chengdu. Of course, no visit to this city is complete without seeing giant pandas and enjoying a cup of tea while getting your ears cleaned in the iconic People's Park. After all, when in Chengdu, do as the locals do.

After all, when in Chengdu, do as the locals do.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.3
programme.desc: Hainan- the anchor-point of China's southern frontier, its islands custodians of the vast South China Sea! And perhaps nothing embodies the allure of the oceanic wonderland more than the limpid waters of Sansha. From volcanic northern shores to cutting-edge modern cities, join us on coastal journey dotted with historic lighthouses illuminating a fascinating past and guiding the way to a promising future...

programme.title: Culture Forward
programme.desc: Culture Forward is set at a pivotal moment between the close of China’s 14th Five-Year Plan and the launch of the 15th. The program explores how technology is giving culture new momentum, featuring cases such as Guizhou’s mechanized batik techniques, the digital presentation of Liangzhu culture, AI-powered design for Miao embroidery, and creative innovation at the Hunan Museum. Through real-world practices, it reveals how traditional Chinese culture is being revitalized in the age of intelligence—moving toward a future that is more open, innovative, and high-quality.

programme.title: Awakening Lion
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: The Story of Flying Trident
programme.desc: Feicha, or a flying trident, is a type of performance where you balance a revolving trident on your body. In Chinese history, the trident was one of the 18 main weapons of war, but now it has become more of an acrobatic act. For thousands of years, people in China have practiced it as a performance and also as a way to keep fit.It seems like the trident is stuck to the person’s body like a magnet, but that effect is achieved by tensing and relaxing muscles at the right time so that the movement of the trident is channeled in the right direction—be it around the hands, neck, or back. Otherwise, its energy will turn against the performer.

programme.title: The Story of Flying Trident

Otherwise, its energy will turn against the performer.

programme.title: World Heritages in Handan
programme.desc: Handan, a renowned historical and cultural city in China, boasts abundant cultural and tourism resources, including the Handan Museum and Congtai Park in Congtai District, as well as the Xiangtangshan Grottoes, Guangfu Ancient City, Cizhou Kiln, etc. Handan also has a long-standing connection with the Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal, a world cultural heritage. As a great project created in ancient China, the Grand Canal has not only exerted a profound impact on cities along the route in terms of transportation, economy and culture, but also served as an important driving force for Handan's development.

programme.title: World Heritages in Handan
programme.desc: Handan, a renowned historical and cultural city in China, boasts abundant cultural and tourism resources, including the Handan Museum and Congtai Park in Congtai District, as well as the Xiangtangshan Grottoes, Guangfu Ancient City, Cizhou Kiln, etc. Handan also has a long-standing connection with the Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal, a world cultural heritage. As a great project created in ancient China, the Grand Canal has not only exerted a profound impact on cities along the route in terms of transportation, economy and culture, but also served as an important driving force for Handan's development.

programme.title: China Untapped: Changsha
programme.desc: The city of Changsha is one of central China's powerhouses. It's a leader in both the construction machinery industry and in media – Changsha was China's first UNESCO "Creative City". It's also one of China's best-known cities for its nightlife. The soul of a city is in its people, and this episode focuses on the women of Changsha who are helping to innovate, change and further develop this city they call home.

programme.title: China Untapped: Changsha

The soul of a city is in its people, and this episode focuses on the women of Changsha who are helping to innovate, change and further develop this city they call home.

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. Guided by the concept of "lucid waters and lush mountains are invaluable assets", Huzhou now is committed to developing green industries. In this episode, CGTN hosts Tianran He and Hannah Wilson travelled to Huzhou, where they tried a variety of guesthouse hotels in the Moganshan Mountains; experienced the life of digital nomads in Anji’s countryside; learned about the eco-concepts behind China's traditional architectures in Nanxun Ancient Town; and explored the development of eco-agriculture powered by high-tech along...

programme.title: China Bound：Xiamen
programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

The program explores how technology is giving culture new momentum, featuring cases such as Guizhou’s mechanized batik techniques, the digital presentation of Liangzhu culture, AI-powered design for Miao embroidery, and creative innovation at the Hunan Museum. Through real-world practices, it reveals how traditional Chinese culture is being revitalized in the age of intelligence—moving toward a future that is more open, innovative, and high-quality.

programme.title: Culture Forward
programme.desc: Culture Forward is set at a pivotal moment between the close of China’s 14th Five-Year Plan and the launch of the 15th. The program explores how technology is giving culture new momentum, featuring cases such as Guizhou’s mechanized batik techniques, the digital presentation of Liangzhu culture, AI-powered design for Miao embroidery, and creative innovation at the Hunan Museum. Through real-world practices, it reveals how traditional Chinese culture is being revitalized in the age of intelligence—moving toward a future that is more open, innovative, and high-quality.

programme.title: Awakening Lion
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: The Story of Flying Trident
programme.desc: Feicha, or a flying trident, is a type of performance where you balance a revolving trident on your body. In Chinese history, the trident was one of the 18 main weapons of war, but now it has become more of an acrobatic act. For thousands of years, people in China have practiced it as a performance and also as a way to keep fit.It seems like the trident is stuck to the person’s body like a magnet, but that effect is achieved by tensing and relaxing muscles at the right time so that the movement of the trident is channeled in the right direction—be it around the hands, neck, or back. Otherwise, its energy will turn against the performer.

programme.title: The Story of Flying Trident

Otherwise, its energy will turn against the performer.

programme.title: World Heritages in Handan
programme.desc: Handan, a renowned historical and cultural city in China, boasts abundant cultural and tourism resources, including the Handan Museum and Congtai Park in Congtai District, as well as the Xiangtangshan Grottoes, Guangfu Ancient City, Cizhou Kiln, etc. Handan also has a long-standing connection with the Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal, a world cultural heritage. As a great project created in ancient China, the Grand Canal has not only exerted a profound impact on cities along the route in terms of transportation, economy and culture, but also served as an important driving force for Handan's development.

programme.title: World Heritages in Handan

programme.title: China Untapped: Changsha
programme.desc: The city of Changsha is one of central China's powerhouses. It's a leader in both the construction machinery industry and in media – Changsha was China's first UNESCO "Creative City". It's also one of China's best-known cities for its nightlife. The soul of a city is in its people, and this episode focuses on the women of Changsha who are helping to innovate, change and further develop this city they call home.

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. Guided by the concept of "lucid waters and lush mountains are invaluable assets", Huzhou now is committed to developing green industries. In this episode, CGTN hosts Tianran He and Hannah Wilson travelled to Huzhou, where they tried a variety of guesthouse hotels in the Moganshan Mountains; experienced the life of digital nomads in Anji’s countryside; learned about the eco-concepts behind China's traditional architectures in Nanxun Ancient Town; and explored the development of eco-agriculture powered by high-tech along...

programme.title: China Bound：Xiamen
programme.desc: What's it like in one of China's happiest cities? Tianran is in sunny Xiamen on China's southeast coast, visiting the city's famous "island of music" and sampling local delicacies like shark meatballs, shacha noodles and fish in "soy sauce water". He'll discover former fishing villages turned hipster havens, drink tea with locals beneath traditional tenement buildings, and visit a student enclave brimming with creative startup energy.

programme.title: China Bound：Suzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He visits Suzhou, a historic city in eastern China famously admired by Marco Polo. Crisscrossed by canals, Suzhou is renowned for its exquisite gardens, where ancient scholars recreated nature's beauty within their own homes. Tianran discovers a city of refined tastes, where even a bowl of breakfast noodles comes with unspoken etiquette, locals insist on only in-season produce, and foodies eagerly await the arrival of prized hairy crabs.

programme.title: China Bound: Chengdu
programme.desc: Tianran spends 3 days in Chengdu, the capital of Sichuan Province and UNESCO's first City of Gastronomy. He dives deep into Chengdu culture, exploring the infamous "mala" numbing and spicy flavors of Sichuanese street food and hotpot, and visiting historical attractions like Wuhou Shrine, Du Fu's Thatched Cottage, and the Jinsha Site Museum, which houses exquisite relics from a 3,000-year-old kingdom uncovered beneath the streets of Chengdu. Of course, no visit to this city is complete without seeing giant pandas and enjoying a cup of tea while getting your ears cleaned in the iconic People's Park. After all, when in Chengdu, do as the locals do.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.3
programme.desc: Hainan- the anchor-point of China's southern frontier, its islands custodians of the vast South China Sea! And perhaps nothing embodies the allure of the oceanic wonderland more than the limpid waters of Sansha. From volcanic northern shores to cutting-edge modern cities, join us on coastal journey dotted with historic lighthouses illuminating a fascinating past and guiding the way to a promising future...

programme.title: China Bound: Chongqing
programme.desc: Join us in southwest China's Chongqing, one of the world's biggest cities. From futuristic skylines and cyberpunk streets, to fire-breathing opera, spicy hotpot and kungfu tea, we're diving deep into this megacity the size of Austria.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Quanzhou Nanyin: A Living Fossil in the History of Chinese Music
programme.desc: Nanyin, also known as “Quanzhou Nanyin,” is an ancient Han Chinese musical tradition whose singing style preserves the time-honored ethnic vocal techniques predating the Tang Dynasty, earning it the title of “a living fossil in the history of Chinese music.”The program features Yegor, a Ukrainian music enthusiast, visiting Master Su Shuyong, a Nanyin inheritor, to hear her decades-long stories of teaching this art. They also tour the Quanzhou Nanyin Troupe, where, led by its artistic director Zhuang Lifen, they learn about the basic performance formats and instruments of Nanyin. Additionally, the episode follows a post-1990s Nanyin performer, Zheng Mingming, as she sips tea with guests...

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The Elegance of Luoyang
programme.desc: Luoyang, as the eastern starting point of the Silk Road, is a city with a rich history and a tradition of cultural inclusivity. As such, it enjoys a prominent position in the heritage of the whole world.In this episode, we’ll visit White Horse Temple to learn about its fascinating international background. At Longmen Grottoes, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year 2000, we’ll appreciate how the unique ancient splendor is being revived by 3D technology. At the city’s annual Peony Festival, we’ll explore the significance of China’s national flower and national dress.Invited to a Luoyang Water Banquet, we’ll learn something about the city’s culinary traditions....

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China

From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Quanzhou Nanyin: A Living Fossil in the History of Chinese Music
programme.desc: Nanyin, also known as “Quanzhou Nanyin,” is an ancient Han Chinese musical tradition whose singing style preserves the time-honored ethnic vocal techniques predating the Tang Dynasty, earning it the title of “a living fossil in the history of Chinese music.”The program features Yegor, a Ukrainian music enthusiast, visiting Master Su Shuyong, a Nanyin inheritor, to hear her decades-long stories of teaching this art. They also tour the Quanzhou Nanyin Troupe, where, led by its artistic director Zhuang Lifen, they learn about the basic performance formats and instruments of Nanyin. Additionally, the episode follows a post-1990s Nanyin performer, Zheng Mingming, as she sips tea with guests...

From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Bound: Wuhan
programme.desc: Tianran He visits Wuhan, a megacity at the crossroads of central China. From experiencing its vibrant breakfast culture and iconic dishes like hot dry noodles to exploring the lakes and hills of the expansive East Lake, as well as historic districts that showcase a blend of Chinese and Western architecture, Tianran uncovers why Wuhan has been a pivotal city in Chinese history for centuries. Finally, he relives some of these moments through immersive theater performances set amongst historic buildings and aboard a steamship cruising the Yangtze River.

programme.title: China Bound: Harbin
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Harbin, the "Ice City", host of the 2025 Asian Winter Games. Internationally renowned for its breathtaking ice and snow sculptures, the city thrives with activity despite it -20°C winters. From savoring the famous local ice cream and hearty northeastern Chinese dishes such as stewed goose and sweet-and-sour pork, to drifting on the frozen Songhua River and carving down the slopse of Yabuli Ski Resort, discover how much fun you can have in Harbin.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise.Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake...

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.4
programme.desc: Shaanxi, almost right in the middle of China, is a true land of contrasts. South of the Qinling Mountains that divide the province, the terrain has all the allure of south China, while north of this great dividing range lies the Guanzhong Plain and the austere and otherworldly Loess Plateau. Join us for a journey into a province that presents two equally beautiful and fascinating faces to the world!

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes.In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Quanzhou Nanyin: A Living Fossil in the History of Chinese Music
programme.desc: Nanyin, also known as “Quanzhou Nanyin,” is an ancient Han Chinese musical tradition whose singing style preserves the time-honored ethnic vocal techniques predating the Tang Dynasty, earning it the title of “a living fossil in the history of Chinese music.”The program features Yegor, a Ukrainian music enthusiast, visiting Master Su Shuyong, a Nanyin inheritor, to hear her decades-long stories of teaching this art. They also tour the Quanzhou Nanyin Troupe, where, led by its artistic director Zhuang Lifen, they learn about the basic performance formats and instruments of Nanyin. Additionally, the episode follows a post-1990s Nanyin performer, Zheng Mingming, as she sips tea with guests...

programme.title: Jingdezhen Handmade Porcelain
programme.desc: Porcelain has shaped the culture of the whole world. The wisdom behind each of 72 procedures has been refined from generation to generation. The times may change, some techniques may change, but what remains the same is the burning flame of the kiln that keeps setting so many hearts on fire for this ancient craft.

programme.title: The Elegance of Luoyang
programme.desc: Luoyang, as the eastern starting point of the Silk Road, is a city with a rich history and a tradition of cultural inclusivity. As such, it enjoys a prominent position in the heritage of the whole world.In this episode, we’ll visit White Horse Temple to learn about its fascinating international background. At Longmen Grottoes, which were designated as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the year 2000, we’ll appreciate how the unique ancient splendor is being revived by 3D technology. At the city’s annual Peony Festival, we’ll explore the significance of China’s national flower and national dress.Invited to a Luoyang Water Banquet, we’ll learn something about the city’s culinary traditions....

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Bird's-eye China Ep.5
programme.desc: Guarded by mountains in southeast China, Jiangxi channels rivers into Poyang Lake---the Yangtze's vital link. Starting from Mount Lu's misty slopes, watch and marvel as northern plains transition into an undulating land of hills and basins...

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting

programme.title: Ding Porcelain Firing
programme.desc: In ancient times, skilled craftsmen combined clay with fire and, using nothing more than their hands and wisdom, created the history of porcelain.A thousand years ago, at the time of the Northern Song Dynasty, a type of porcelain was produced that stood out for its exquisite quality, elegant coloring and beautiful patterns. This was Ding porcelain, and it became a favorite at the imperial court. However, within 200 years, amid the chaos of constant warfare, the art of making it was lost. The dazzling white light of the Ding kilns had faded, seemingly forever. And yet, 800 years later, that light has been reignited.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council.The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been...

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port.With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads.In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites.Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its...

programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Staying zen on the subway platform
programme.desc: This exercise will allow you to sink deep into nature, helping you as you wait for your train or subway, to relax and escape making the time pass quickly by.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 2 - Take a little nap…
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Take a little nap…
programme.desc: Thanks to this session, after getting into a calm and peaceful rhythm of breathing, you can settle down in your own dream place and take a pleasant and refreshing nap…

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 12 - Managing your kids’ homework
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Managing your kids’ homework
programme.desc: This exercise will help you find some solutions so that homework doesn’t become a source of conflict.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 3 - USA: The end of oil - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 USA: The end of oil - Part 1
programme.desc: "The end of oil" is the amazing story of a raw material which has revolutionized man's life in just a century. But today, oil reserves are depleting. In his crazy race for always more production, Man didn't manage to preserve this precious energy that took million years for the Earth to give out. To tell the adventure of oil, Yann Arthus-Bertrand and his team went to the United States, the country which depends the most on black gold. At the start of the 21st century, it is now urgent to realize that oil resources are limited. It's today that we have to act and get organized, in order to prepare the new era to come. While all our economies are based on the petrol barrel's price, we'll...

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - A la carte grills
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 A la carte grills
programme.desc: If you're a nature enthusiast and a fan of outdoor cooking, the upcoming season is perfect for barbecue enthusiasts! Our journey will take us to Aube, nestled in the heart of the Brévonnes forest, and then to the Massif des Bauges, where grilling and snow create a unique combination. Afterward, we'll explore the Libournais region, where we'll uncover cooking techniques that make use of vine shoots, which are plentiful in the area. Just a short distance from the vineyards, we'll arrive at another corner of Gironde, the Blaye region, where the cooking method remains consistent but features a distinctly local ingredient: eel.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Cryotherapy & Vinyasa Yoga
programme.desc: It's in an episode full of surprise that we meet with Val Khal in the heart of Los Angeles for a visit to the new trendy wellbeing boutique Springs. This new concept store offers the opportunity to make Vinyasa yoga wearing a trendy outfit, treat yourself to a revitalizing juice detox before heading to a Cryotherapy session without leaving the venue! For a relaxing time after an intensive Vinyasa yoga session, Val takes you to a session of Fire cupping where the heath is ideal for getting rid of muscular pains from day-to-day stress.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 2 - Acro Yoga on a Skyscraper
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Acro Yoga on a Skyscraper
programme.desc: Val takes us to Los Angeles, on the roof of one of the most prestigious hotels in Beverly Hills for a session of acro yoga, aerial acrobatic yoga. Then, in the heart of Downtown, Val will experience a glass slide down one of the tallest towers in California. She'll also try SandBox Fitness, the trendy indoor surfing class, before trying Lazerfit, a new way to work out that combines warm-up, stretching and laser tag.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Cutting a tomato plant
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Cutting a tomato plant
programme.desc: We meet a in the market garden of Romainville to continue gardening together. Since we've been gardening, we've already put a lot of different plants in our vegetable garden: herbs, strawberry plants and, a few episodes ago, we even learned how to combine certain plants in the garden. Come on, let's see how they have grown.

programme.desc: Today, we're preparing a pasta salad with pickled peppers. Pasta, tomatoes, carrots and Parmesan are combined with pickled peppers for a tangy, fragrant touch, tomatoes and baby greens. A seasoning of olive oil, garlic and rosemary enhances this fresh, gourmet dish.

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Mashed potatoes with vegetable gravy

programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare mashed potatoes in vegetable stock. The mashed potatoes are soft and melt-in-the-mouth, while the richly flavored broth of bell bell pepper, carrot, zucchini, celeriac and tomato enhances the whole.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Daniel Hautot
programme.desc: He had a real professional rise, quickly moving from commercial to sales manager. But the awareness quickly catches up with him too: what is the purpose of all this? Is it a real pursuit of happiness? Daniel keeps his acquired skills and puts them to the benefit of his passion for yoga. Follow Daniel until the opening of his yoga & community space.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Guillaume Nivelle
programme.desc: To make a profession representative of his personality is the discovery made by Guillaume at twenty-six. After several years as a bartender and crossing a dark period, Guillaume discovers nature and falls in love with it. Accompany Guillaume in his passion, in the middle of a refitted nature.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laura Belfadla
programme.desc: Laura will not tell you the whole truth because she does not want a system where you are told what to do. After several years as head of business development in law firm, Laura changes her life and creates the plant academy. Let yourself go for a healthier life.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Laurence Sala
programme.desc: It took a burnout and several miscarriages to Laurence to take a new path. It's finally a bad thing for a good. Throughout her ordeals, Laurence will know how to listen to her needs, her desires and her passions. With a surprise, follow Laurence in her life as a mother and naturopath.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 4 - Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Nature’s Heroes: Bangladesh - Part 2
programme.desc: With Bangladesh as a running thread, Yann Arthus-Bertrand meets several people who have changed their lives to dedicate themselves to a major project. In Bangladesh, Yves and Runa Marre have created the Friendship Foundation. Among other things, they’ve created a floating hospital installed on an old barge bought in France. Julie Andersen, an ex-advertising executive from New York, has come to South Africa to defend sharks with her Shark Angels Foundation. In North India, Kartik Satyanarayan is helping to stop the dreadful exploitation of the lippus bears (dancing bears). He’s created Wildlife SOS, a preserve to rehabilitate the bears that have been rescued and to give work to the men of...

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Life'n'One
programme.desc: In this episode, Gabriel will start her day with some vegan cuisine. She will experience facial yoga at the Life'n'One holistic wellness center, before meeting Katrin from Active Escapes for a Wake surfing session in Dubai Marina.

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Al Qasr
programme.desc: Gabriela starts her day with a boat ride through the canals of the majestic Jumeirah Al Qasr Hotel. She then meets with chef Gabi for a healthy cooking class, as well as a fitness influencer with whom she will go on a fitness tour. She will end her day with a Thai massage in the hotel's beautiful, lush garden.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Seashells on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Seashells on the menu
programme.desc: We will start this journey near La Rochelle to learn a little more about the breeding of the famous bouchot mussels but also a very particular technique to cook them. We will then go on a tour of France's best oysters: towards Cancale, Arcachon, Montpellier and finally Oléron to learn about the different breeding methods and to find out more about all the species in France. Julie will cook a fricassee of berniques, the little Chinese hats that cover the rocks of the coast, as well as pink clams that are little known to the public. We will continue our journey in Finistère to meet Sylvain, the only abalone breeder in the country, and then to Méru where there are still workshops magnifying...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Matcha Box
programme.desc: Val Kahl is taking you to discover the newest innovation in town for rejuvenation, body cleansing, and boost the body nutrition. She visits Matcha Box: the shops where you can find all drinks and food using “matcha” as the base ingredients. After trying out the refreshing matcha beverages, Val passes by the Willow Spa to experience the famous Enzyme Bath. Before stepping into the hot bath full of enzyme, Val took a sip of enzyme and ginger tea to detoxify and relax the muscles!

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Women Kundalini Yoga
programme.desc: It's an expedition in the heart of nature that is waiting for you during this day with Val Kahl. This journey begins at the top of Mount Ojai for a Kundalini Yoga session with the organization Women Kundalini Yoga. This breathtaking view of the region will allow you to fully grasp your emotions and embrace the positive energy of this group of spiritual women. Finally, the botanical garden of Santa Barbara will open up to you so to admire the well preserved variety of Californian flora as you make your way through the first botanical salon in the USA.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - How to install a plant in the garden
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 How to install a plant in the garden
programme.desc: Today, we're meeting in the market garden of Romainville to learn how to install a plant in the garden. In our last episode, we saw how to plant a small seed to make it germinate and give a plant, it’s called a "seedling". To go faster, you can also buy or collect small plants that you will install in your garden. That's what we'll see today.

programme.title: Minute Bowl - Season 1 - Acaï Bowl
programme.desc: In this episode we decided to make an Acai bowl. For this you will need frozen bananas, milk, acai powder, kiwi, berries, chia seeds and edible flowers. Acai is a small berry, a remarkable concentrate of antioxidants that help fight against premature aging of cells and thus prevent the onset of cardiovascular disease.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Yesmin
programme.desc: Born in Mexico but always attracted to new cultures and countries, Yesmin worked many jobs until she finally found her calling. She decided to merge her passions for art and travel and became a “Vagabond Artist”. She is now able to travel the world while making a living by painting beautiful murals in her own distinct style.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Jessica
programme.desc: Jessica’s life as a teenager was marked by a family tragedy that she struggled to cope with for a long time. Her anxiety had reached an all-time high and she was experiencing regular panic attacks when finally, she discovered surfing. This newfound passion thoroughly changed her lifestyle, ultimately resulting in a move to Bali, Indonesia. Today, she runs mindful surf & yoga retreats while also working online as a wellness writer.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Lisa & Pete
programme.desc: Two urbanites surprise themselves by changing their city lifestyles for farm living : Lisa, an architect based in London, and her husband Pete, an actor seen in Hollywoodian movies such as “Ghost in the Shell” alongside Scarlett Johansson and a rock star in his own right in his native Malaysia. Together, they figured they were far from being ready for retirement. Instead, they dove headfirst into running an organic farm where they now both thrive while taking pleasure in returning to a simpler life.

programme.title: Ways to School - Season 1 - Jose Alfredo and Angel, Mexico
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Jose Alfredo and Angel, Mexico
programme.desc: Jose Alfredo, 12, has been blind from birth. But that doesn't stop him from living in one of the world's biggest cities: Mexico City. The dangers of moving around in such a metropolis are exacerbated for a blind person. Despite this, Jose Alfredo embarks on a 2-hour journey every day, relying solely on his intuition, his cane, and the help of his little brother.

programme.title: Ways to School - Season 1 - Obdulia, Mexico
programme.desc: Obdulia González Fierro is 9 years old, she lives with her parents and brothers in the imposing and desolate Sierra Tarahumara. To get to the religious boarding school in which she receives a formal education, Obdulia and her friend Irma have to travel 6 hours. They walk for 3 hours and spend 3 more on a truck. Along the way they encounter dangers like poisonous snakes and unstable trails in mountains that are over 2,000 meters high.

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 1
programme.desc: Animals represent 2/3 of the species on earth. We are increasingly concerned about our impact on the world. But what place does man have for the wild animal? Discover how it is possible to reintroduce wild animals in South Africa, or the disaster that took place with the sea lions in Namibia, and also how to preserve rhinos or other animals in biodiversity. There, women and men manage to protect wildlife every day.

programme.title: My Day Dubai - Season 1 - Le Royal Meridien
programme.desc: In this episode, our host Gabriela will spend her day at the beautiful Royal Meridien. She'll learn new recipes with Chef Jorge at Brasserie 2.0. She'll enjoy a very special facial at the hotel's spa, before taking wakeboarding lessons with Coach Mike at sunset.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Lamb flavors
programme.desc: Tender and tasty, lamb plays a key role in festive cuisine but also in everyday cuisine. Julie sets off on the French roads to discover the different breeds existing across the country. It must be said that France has one of the most varied productions in the world. There are more than forty breeds listed in 5 categories. But more than the breed, it is the way in which they are fed that interests connoisseurs. Julie introduces us to producers and cooks of this refined dish!

After doing a bit of market sightseeing along the way, she heads out to Amazebowls where she can’t resist making the famous Acai Bowl for herself!

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Pilates on Trampoline
programme.desc: It's in Newport, Orange County, that we're going for a pilates on trampoline class with the uplifting company Bouncing Society. This acrobatic dance will full you with energy as you work on your legs and take a deep breath before the next class. This will take place at the shop RA 90 which offers circle yoga’s classes that will work your body fully, all using only rubber.

programme.title: My First Garden - Season 1 - Plant associations
programme.desc: Today we will see how plants can be combined in the garden. Plants are like people: they like to mix and some are even real friends and love to grow together. We will show you a combination of three plants and explain why it is smart to combine these three plants in your vegetable garden.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Ika
programme.desc: After graduating with a Law degree, Ika built a career as a legal manager and advisor. For a decade she worked on risk-related matters. One day, she decided to take a big calculated risk of her own and give up her law-life to pursue a passion for jewelry-making. Her creations are inspired by her travels and the natural materials surrounding her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Hansen
programme.desc: A former model and Sports News anchor, Hansen has drifted away from the limelight to focus on a personal journey. His Yoga trip began 10 years ago, after suffering a herniated disc injury. His experiences have taught him that the only way one can truly change the world is to raise the consciousness of humanity, and this actually starts within ourselves.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Mark, Camille & Juliana
programme.desc: A Filipino couple with a shared love for traveling decide to change their lives and explore a passion for eco-tourism. Their glamping tents are perched on the hills of Bacuit Bay and promise a natural and enriching experience. Daily sunset yoga sessions and Farm-to-Table dining are just some of the activities they share with their guests. Their energy attracts like-minded people on similar journeys, notably their yoga teacher Juliana who has an inspiring story of her own to share with us.

programme.title: My Zen Family - Season 1 - Last preps
programme.desc: Clara and Gianni’s boys are born! But they came in a little bit early, and have been admitted in neonatal resuscitation... Coralie and Florent are still walking on clouds as they wait for their daughter, Lena. So are Nathalie and Naoufel, who arrange a tour of the future baby’s bedroom. Things are still tense between Kim and her parents. Her father blames here for having left school too early and the family makes no effort to hide their dislike of Pierre, the would-be father, who is still a no-show. They go as far as opposing Kim’s preferred choice for the future boy’s name, since it was his father that came up with the idea...

programme.title: My Zen Family - Season 1 - Welcome in a new world
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Welcome in a new world
programme.desc: All the babies are born! How are Clara and Gianni coping with the situation with two newborn children at home? Does this help them to mourn the loss of their unborn second baby? Was Coralie allowed her much-desired epidural? Did Pierre came back at Kim’s side to finally act as the father, and what name did Kim settle upon for her son? In the end, it is Nathalie and Naoufel, our most experienced parents, who share with us some words of wisdom: “one isn’t just born being a parent, one has to learn it.”

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 2 - Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Do wild animals still exist ? - Part 2
programme.desc: Yann Arthus-Bertrand surprises us with preserved landscapes. And sometimes even with vast spaces where man's imprint remains invisible. Yann Arthus-Bertrand continues his world tour in favour of the protection of the environment and wildlife. From the tip of the African continent, he decides to take down the fences to let the animals roam freely, then he takes us to Cambodia to see if the tigers will disappear. Then we head to London to follow the chimpanzee or to France to study and save the bees.

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 5 - Love & open heart
programme.desc: The aim of this session is to help you open up to others by visualizing those times in the past when others opened up to you. You'll be guided throughout the entire exercise so that in the end, you'll be able to learn from your past experience and open up to others in a similar way.
